name: La Canal de Navarrés — Circular by the Albufera de Anna to the Piscina de Bolbaite
name_original: La Canal de Navarrés — Circular por la Albufera de Anna hasta la Piscina de Bolbaite
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Anna are characterized by a diverse landscape featuring lakes, rivers, and natural pools, set amidst rolling terrain. The region offers a mix of natural monuments and scenic views, with routes often traversing areas with moderate to significant elevation changes. Cyclists can explore the natural beauty of the area, encountering features like the Albufera de Anna and the Sellent River. Best touring cycling routes around Anna The most popular touring cycling route is La Canal de Navarrés — Circular by the Albufera de Anna to the Piscina de Bolbaite, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Albufera de Anna and leads to the Piscina de Bolbaite. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bridge over the Sellent River – Albufera de Anna loop from Anna, a difficult 34.5 miles (55.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and explores the area around the Sellent River and Albufera de Anna. Local touring cyclists also love the Anna – Albufera de Anna loop from Anna, a 32.4 miles (52.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with substantial climbs, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Anna is defined by lakes, rivers, and natural pools. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with considerable elevation. The routes in Anna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Anna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Anna?
A: There are 16 touring cycling routes available around Anna, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Anna?
A: The touring cycling routes around Anna feature a diverse landscape with lakes, rivers, and natural pools, set amidst rolling terrain. You'll encounter areas with moderate to significant elevation changes, providing both scenic views and challenging climbs. The region is known for its natural monuments and varied topography.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Anna?
A: While many routes in Anna are challenging, there is one easy touring cycling route available. For those looking for a moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919308020/la-canal-de-navarres-circular-by-the-albufera-de-anna-to-the-piscina-de-bolbaite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canal de Navarrés — Circular by the Albufera de Anna to the Piscina de Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.6-mile trail that offers views of the Albufera de Anna and leads to the Piscina de Bolbaite.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Anna?
A: Yes, Anna is well-suited for experienced touring cyclists, with 12 out of 16 routes classified as difficult. These routes often feature substantial climbs and significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14665990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anna – Albufera de Anna loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt;, a 32.4-mile trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Anna?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Anna are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919308020/la-canal-de-navarres-circular-by-the-albufera-de-anna-to-the-piscina-de-bolbaite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canal de Navarrés — Circular by the Albufera de Anna to the Piscina de Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31535193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Sellent River – Albufera de Anna loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often traverse areas rich in natural beauty. You can explore features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt;, the Sellent River, and various natural pools. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31525865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool – Anna loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt;, will take you directly past stunning waterfalls and gorges.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Anna offers several interesting landmarks. You can find historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2101633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Basilica of Santa María (La Seu), Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; are accessible.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Anna?
A: The touring cycling routes in Anna are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the presence of lakes, rivers, and natural pools, and the challenging yet rewarding terrain.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919308020/la-canal-de-navarres-circular-by-the-albufera-de-anna-to-the-piscina-de-bolbaite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canal de Navarrés — Circular by the Albufera de Anna to the Piscina de Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes, while more difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14665990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anna – Albufera de Anna loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt; often take around 4 hours 35 minutes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer stunning views of the landscape?
A: Many routes in Anna provide stunning views, particularly those that traverse the rolling hills and pass by natural features. The region's diverse landscape, including lakes, rivers, and natural pools, ensures picturesque scenery throughout your ride. Routes exploring La Canal de Navarrés or the Sellent River are known for their scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can definitely find routes that feature waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31525865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool – Anna loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 36.4-mile route that will take you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in Anna?
A: One of the longest touring cycling routes in Anna is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31525865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool – Anna loop from Anna&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 36.4 miles (58.7 km). This difficult route also features significant elevation gain, making it a substantial ride for experienced cyclists.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with exploring gorges or caves?
A: Absolutely. The region around Anna is home to several gorges and caves that can be explored. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4063316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabrenta Cave&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible near cycling routes, allowing for a varied outdoor experience.
tips:
The lake is a protected area of flora and fauna. Even so, it has parking lots and a recreational area.

It is an ideal place to spend a quiet day with the family.

Not far from the town of Anna, is located this beautiful spot with a waterfall and a natural pool. It is located on the water route and can be visited both by bicycle and on foot. An ideal place to visit all year round!

From here, you get the best views of Salto de Chella and the valley. A very nice area with easy access.

I encourage you to descend on foot or by bicycle to the foot of the waterfall. It is worth visiting!

Unmissable! Natural water pool fed by the Sellent River. An incredible swimming spot with a picnic area and parking. You can walk or bike to the pool from the village of Bolbaite, which has various facilities. There's a walking trail along the river that leads to the waterfalls that feed the pool.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of the Salto de Chella
